commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Codex auth migration to skip Codex auth.json API-key imports when OAuth is present, adds models status stale-key warnings, and updates tests plus CHANGELOG.

PR surface: Source +104, Tests +144, Docs +1. Total +249 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the main blocking regression: source inspection shows current main imports both OAuth and API-key credentials from Codex auth.json without auth_mode gating, while this PR ignores the API key whenever auth_mode is absent or not apikey. No tests were run because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Protected maintainer-labeled draft with auth-provider compatibility and overlapping status-command work needs author/maintainer revision, not cleanup close or automated repair.

target.title: Warn about stale OpenAI keys with Codex auth
target.body:
## Summary
- Skip importing `OPENAI_API_KEY` from Codex `auth.json` when ChatGPT OAuth is present, while preserving explicit `auth_mode: "apikey"` sources.
- Add `models status` JSON and terminal warnings when OpenAI models are routed through Codex OAuth but direct OpenAI API-key auth remains configured as a possible stale fallback.
- Add regression coverage for Codex migration and the status warning, plus an unreleased changelog entry.

## Root Cause
The local incident had two overlapping inputs: OpenClaw stored `openai:*` API-key profiles from `models auth paste-api-key`, and the shell also exported `OPENAI_API_KEY`. Session and cron logs showed the failing run used direct `provider=openai`, `api=openai-responses`, `model=gpt-5.5` and returned OpenAI platform `insufficient_quota`; after removing the OpenAI API-key profile/order and using Codex OAuth, the same agent ran as `provider=openai-codex`, `api=openai-codex-responses`.

The durable product issue is that OpenClaw intentionally allows OpenAI API-key credentials to coexist with Codex OAuth as backup auth. That makes valid fallback behavior possible, but it also lets stale platform keys survive a Codex auth migration or re-auth and produce confusing platform quota failures.

## Validation
- `pnpm test extensions/codex/src/migration/provider.test.ts src/commands/models/list.status.test.ts`
- `pnpm build`
- `git diff --check`
target.author: amknight
